---
title: "Reproducing figure 5a"
author: "Harchit Kaur"
date: today 
project: 
  output-dir: Output
  execute-dir: project
toc: true 
format: 
  html: 
    embed-resources: true
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
editor: visual 
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
execute:
  warning: false 
  message: false
  freeze: false 
---

## Prepare Session

```{r}
#| label: libraries and data import 
#| echo: false


library(ROCR)
#library(MetaIntegrator)
library(ggplot2)
library(parallel)
library(OptimalCutpoints)
library(data.table)
library(xgboost)
library(caret)
library(dplyr)
library(pROC)
library(e1071) #SVM
library(nnet) #MLP 


df <- read.csv("Data/processed/41698_2025_1043_MOESM3_ESM.csv")

```

```{r}
#| label: clean dataset

#create outcome variable y
df$y <- ifelse(df$group == "lung cancer", 1, 0)
df$y <- as.factor(df$y)

#clean smoking variable 
df$smoking_binary <- ifelse(df$smokingstatus == "never", 0, 1)

#compute AUC function 

get_auc <- function(pred, truth) {
  roc_obj <- roc(truth, pred)
  
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  ci_vals <- ci.auc(roc_obj)
  
  data.frame(
    auc = auc_val,
    lower_ci = ci_vals[1],
    upper_ci = ci_vals[3]
  )
}
```

## Fit models

```{r}
#| label: build models

m1 <- glm(y ~ luncancerscore, data = df, family = "binomial")
p1 <- predict(m1, type = "response")
r1 <- get_auc(p1, df$y)
r1$model <- "lung cancer score"

m2 <- glm(y ~ age, data = df, family = "binomial")
p2 <- predict(m2, type = "response")
r2 <- get_auc(p2, df$y)
r2$model <- "age"


m3 <- glm(y ~ smoking_binary, data = df, family = "binomial")
p3 <- predict(m3, type = "response")
r3 <- get_auc(p3, df$y)
r3$model <- "smoking"


m4 <- glm(y ~ age + smoking_binary, data = df, family = "binomial")
p4 <- predict(m4, type = "response")
r4 <- get_auc(p4, df$y)
r4$model <- "age + smoking"

m5 <- glm(y ~ luncancerscore + age + smoking_binary, data = df, family = "binomial")
p5 <- predict(m5, type = "response")
r5 <- get_auc(p5, df$y)
r5$model <- "lung cancer score + age + smoking"


results <- bind_rows(r1, r2, r3, r4, r5)
results$method <- "logistic regression"



```

## Reproduce Figure 5A

```{r}
#| label: pre-plot with logistic regression

results$model <- factor(results$model, levels = c(
  "lung cancer score",
  "age",
  "smoking",
  "age + smoking",
  "lung cancer score + age + smoking"
))

ggplot(results, aes(x = auc, y = model)) +
  
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower_ci, xmax = upper_ci), height = 0.2) +
  
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levels(results$model))) +
  
  labs(
    title = "AUC of lung cancer vs control/benign (validation UK)",
    x = "AUC",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  
  xlim(0.55, 0.9) +
  
  theme_minimal()
```

## Do it all in one go 

```{r}
#| label: lr-models
# models
lr1 <- glm(y ~ lungcancerscore, data = df, family = "binomial")
lr2 <- glm(y ~ age, data = df, family = "binomial")
lr3 <- glm(y ~ smoking_binary, data = df, family = "binomial")
lr4 <- glm(y ~ age + smoking_binary, data = df, family = "binomial")
lr5 <- glm(y ~ lungcancerscore + age + smoking_binary, data = df, family = "binomial")

# predictions
p_lr1 <- predict(lr1, type = "response")
p_lr2 <- predict(lr2, type = "response")
p_lr3 <- predict(lr3, type = "response")
p_lr4 <- predict(lr4, type = "response")
p_lr5 <- predict(lr5, type = "response")

# results
lr_results <- bind_rows(
  get_auc(p_lr1, df$y) %>% mutate(model = "lung cancer score"),
  get_auc(p_lr2, df$y) %>% mutate(model = "age"),
  get_auc(p_lr3, df$y) %>% mutate(model = "smoking"),
  get_auc(p_lr4, df$y) %>% mutate(model = "age + smoking"),
  get_auc(p_lr5, df$y) %>% mutate(model = "lung cancer score + age + smoking")
)

lr_results$method <- "logistic regression"
```

```{r}
#| label: svm-models 

get_svm_auc <- function(formula, model_name) {
  m <- svm(formula, data = df, probability = TRUE)
  pred <- predict(m, newdata = df, probability = TRUE)
  probs <- attr(pred, "probabilities")
  
  # check column name
  col <- colnames(probs)[2]
  p <- probs[, col]
  
  get_auc(p, df$y) %>%
    mutate(model = model_name, method = "SVM")
}

svm_results <- bind_rows(
  get_svm_auc(y ~ lungcancerscore, "lung cancer score"),
  get_svm_auc(y ~ age, "age"),
  get_svm_auc(y ~ smoking_binary, "smoking"),
  get_svm_auc(y ~ age + smoking_binary, "age + smoking"),
  get_svm_auc(y ~ lungcancerscore + age + smoking_binary,
              "lung cancer score + age + smoking")
)


```

```{r}
#| label: MLP-models

get_mlp_auc <- function(formula, model_name) {
  m <- nnet(formula, data = df, size = 3, trace = FALSE)
  p <- predict(m, type = "raw")
  
  get_auc(p, df$y) %>%
    mutate(model = model_name, method = "MLP")
}

mlp_results <- bind_rows(
  get_mlp_auc(y ~ lungcancerscore, "lung cancer score"),
  get_mlp_auc(y ~ age, "age"),
  get_mlp_auc(y ~ smoking_binary, "smoking"),
  get_mlp_auc(y ~ age + smoking_binary, "age + smoking"),
  get_mlp_auc(y ~ lungcancerscore + age + smoking_binary,
              "lung cancer score + age + smoking")
)

```

```{r}
#| label: combine all results

results_all <- bind_rows(lr_results, svm_results, mlp_results)
```

```{r}
library(forcats)

results_all$model <- fct_rev(factor(results_all$model, levels = c(
  "lung cancer score",
  "age",
  "smoking",
  "age + smoking",
  "lung cancer score + age + smoking"
)))

ggplot(results_all, aes(x = auc, y = model, color = method)) +
  
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 3) +
  
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = lower_ci, xmax = upper_ci),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.5),
    height = 0.2
  ) +
  
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "logistic regression" = "black",
    "SVM" = "#1f77b4",
    "MLP" = "#9467bd"
  )) +
  
  labs(
    title = "AUC of lung cancer vs control/benign (validation UK)",
    x = "AUC",
    y = NULL,
    color = NULL
  ) +
  
  xlim(0.55, 0.9) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12)
  )
```

We can see that in full model the MLP is shifted towards left that is because we trained the model on the data set we have and not like in the paper. So the model is instable. It is not biological. Let me try to fix this.
